commands.review_summary:
This PR changes embedded agent model-call diagnostics to count streamed text/thinking/tool-call deltas by incremental UTF-8 bytes, omit `partial` from fallback chunk accounting, and add regression tests for both paths.

PR surface: Source +38, Tests +91. Total +129 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main serializes each streamed chunk, and stream delta events carry a cumulative `partial` snapshot. The added throwing-`toJSON` regression would fail before the patch and pass after the incremental delta counter.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review should decide the diagnostic metric compatibility risk and then proceed through normal PR checks; there is no narrow automated repair to request.

target.title: fix(agents): count stream deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary

- Count streamed model response bytes from incremental delta payloads instead of serializing growing `partial` snapshots on every chunk.
- Keep non-delta diagnostic size accounting best-effort and omit `partial` from fallback envelope accounting.
- Add regressions proving diagnostic byte accounting does not touch full partial snapshots and does not break stream delivery when byte inspection fails.

## Linked context

Related https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.
Related https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87671 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/87818.

This targets the diagnostics/full-partial contributor from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599#issuecomment-4555471713. It does not close the full Windows local-provider starvation issue by itself.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: model-call diagnostic byte accounting could repeatedly `JSON.stringify` stream chunks whose `partial` field held the full assistant answer so far, creating repeated work during dense text streams.
- Real environment tested: local Codex worktree for focused regression/static checks, plus AWS Crabbox Linux for the changed gate.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts --reporter=dot

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts

.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local

node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 60m --ttl 120m --timing-json --shell -- "git fetch --unshallow origin || git fetch --deepen=2000 origin main; git fetch origin main:refs/remotes/origin/main; pnpm check:changed"
```

- Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 2 files / 26 tests after the final rebase; oxfmt, oxlint, and diff whitespace checks passed; autoreview returned clean with no accepted/actionable findings; AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed with provider `aws`, lease `cbx_1d741a79db2c`, run `run_16a0aafc9846`, exit 0.
- Observed result after fix: text-delta diagnostics count only the new delta bytes and never invoke serialization on the growing `partial` snapshot; opaque chunk byte-accounting failures are ignored without dropping stream chunks.
- What was not tested: the original Windows local-daemon reproduction from #86599 was not rerun here. This is one fix in the broader event-loop starvation cluster, alongside the already-merged subscriber incremental streaming work and the separate native-Ollama dense-stream yielding PR.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the remote changed gate was run before the final conflict-free rebase onto the newest `origin/main`; after that rebase, the focused regression and static checks were rerun locally and still passed.
- Before evidence: commit c6e98493511b3a18e6f2f402693ef7dc832a071f introduced response stream byte accounting by serializing every streamed chunk, which is expensive when chunks include full `partial` snapshots.

## Tests and validation

Focused regression coverage was added in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts` for both the no-full-partial-serialization path and the best-effort opaque-chunk path.

Commands run are listed in Real behavior proof.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `No`; this changes diagnostic accounting work and the meaning of `responseStreamBytes` for delta chunks to represent incremental response bytes rather than repeated full snapshot envelopes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`.

Highest-risk area: downstream diagnostics consumers that compare `responseStreamBytes` values before/after this patch.

Risk mitigation: the field remains present, monotonic, and best-effort, but no longer scales with duplicated partial snapshots. Request payload byte accounting and terminal output-message capture remain unchanged.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review.

Waiting on: CI after PR creation.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: local autoreview found no accepted/actionable findings.
target.author: vincentkoc
